channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Global markets plunge as Wall Street's woes continue under tariffs
item.description: The global market meltdown comes after a massive sell-off that sent Wall Street into a frenzy late last week.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: H2Go: How experts, industry leaders say US hydrogen is fuel for the future of agriculture, energy, security
item.description: PNWHA president Chris Green said, "Hydrogen has a lot of dexterity as a molecule, and it can be used for a host of different things.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Miami’s ‘Little Venezuela’ fears Trump's moves against migration
item.description: On Feb. 3, the Trump administration ended two federal programs that together allowed more 700,000 Venezuelans to live and work legally in the U.S. It’s all anyone discusses in Doral, Florida, the largest Venezuelan community in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Anti-Trump activist boasts of being 'undocumented, unafraid, queer' at rally
item.description: An undocumented migrant activist declared that she was "queer" and "unafraid" of President Donald Trump during an anti-Trump protest in Washington, D.C., on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State Supreme Courts have become an electoral battleground. But some states choose a different path
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — The race for control of the Wisconsin Supreme Court drew $100 million in campaign spending, attack ads and the attention of President Donald Trump and close ally Elon Musk. While its spending set a record for a U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO and JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: JP Morgan predicts 2025 recession as Trump tariffs rattle market
item.description: President Trump's tariffs and their fallout will cause the U.S. economy to enter a recession this year, J.P. Morgan predicts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department seeks 7 year sentence for George Santos
item.description: Prosecutors said the length of the requested sentence — 87 months — reflects the serious nature of Santos’s “unparalleled” crimes.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Divided Supreme Court sides with Trump to block teacher grants
item.description: A divided Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration by allowing officials to block $65 million in teacher development grants frozen over concerns they were promoting diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks Trump from dismantling agency that funds community groups in Latin American countries
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge agreed on Friday to block the Trump administration from dismantling an independent agency that distributes grant money to community development groups in Latin American and Caribbean countries. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Billionaires Musk and Soros push Wisconsin Supreme Court race spending over $100M
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Spending by billionaires including Elon Musk and George Soros has helped to push total spending on the Wisconsin Supreme Court race won by the Democratic-backed candidate over $100 million, the Brennan Center for Law reported Friday. That is the most ever spent on a U.S.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sex ed in Minnesota schools is up for a rewrite: 4 things to know
item.description: Lawmakers, advocates, students and teachers are working now to update standards for teaching sexual health education in schools. But the topic is uncomfortable and reaching a deal will be a challenge. Here’s where things stand now.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stroozas, MPR News

item.title: A Pentagon inspector general announces investigation into Hegseth’s use of Signal
item.description: The announcement comes after lawmakers voiced concern about whether top national security officials discussed classified information on Signal about a military strike against Houthi rebels in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Despite backlash, Florida county term limit bill advances in the House
item.description: Florida voters may have the chance to set term limits, not just for state lawmakers, but also for county commissioners and school board members.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: After federal firings, officials worry about program that helps many Minnesotans pay for heat
item.description: Minnesota was scheduled to receive an additional $12 million to $13 million in LIHEAP funds to help another 10,000 households. If that payment is delayed, the program could run out of money by mid-April.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis announces support as Florida Senate takes up 'chemtrails' bill
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis expressed his support for a bill banning what he called weather modification "nonsense" before it hits the Senate floor on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Answering your questions about President Trump's vast new tariffs
item.description: After weeks of anticipation and speculation, President Donald Trump followed through on his reciprocal tariff threats by declaring on Wednesday a 10% baseline tax on imports from all countries and higher tariff rates on dozens of nations that run trade surpluses with the United States.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Rugaber And Paul Wiseman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Potential Trump tariff plan could cost households thousands: new analysis
item.description: A new analysis from The Budget Lab at Yale shows how much a broad tariff plan could cost U.S. households.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams federal bribery case dismissed
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams' federal bribery case has been dismissed, court records show. In a letter filed Wednesday, Judge Dale Ho ordered the case dropped with prejudice, meaning the government cannot bring these charges against Adams again in the future.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Ohio school district reverts to birth names, catching students, teachers off guard
item.description: Students at Columbus City Schools with preferred names in the district's system had their names reverted back to match their birth certificates.
item.dc:creator: Delaney Ruth, Fox 5

item.title: Legislation aimed at protecting Florida state parks stuck in committee
item.description: What all started with a leaked proposal to build golf courses and pickleball courts at state parks last year has now resulted in legislation that aims to protect natural grounds from those very acts.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
